commit.message:
Widen TestNowAgreesWithTimeSince tolerance from ±1ms to ±2ms

On the fallback path (amd64) both reads use time.Since but are not
atomic, so scheduler jitter under CI load can cause up to ~2ms skew.

commit.message:
Optimize scroll: send only visible lines + token cache

Three changes to fix laggy scrolling:

1. visibleLines getter: only send viewport lines (~40) to Rust instead
   of ALL file lines. For a 1538-line file this is a 40x reduction in
   per-frame work (renderLine FFI calls + JSON.stringify).

2. Token serialization cache: NativeRenderCoordinator caches serialized
   token JSON per (lineNumber, content). During scroll the line text
   doesn't change, so cache hits avoid re-tokenizing + re-serializing.

3. Clean up _pollEvents return type (number instead of boolean) for
   Perry AOT compatibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Task 73: feat: derive AUTOPILOT_STALE_LOCK_MINUTES from agent timeouts (#78)

* feat: derive AUTOPILOT_STALE_LOCK_MINUTES from agent timeouts

Replace hardcoded 45-minute stale lock default with automatic derivation:
stale_threshold = max(TIMEOUT_CODER, TIMEOUT_FIXER, TIMEOUT_SPEC_REVIEW) / 60 + 5

This prevents race conditions where a coder finishing at the timeout
boundary could have its lock cleaned by stale lock detection.

AUTOPILOT_STALE_LOCK_MINUTES remains as an optional explicit override.
log_effective_config shows the derived value with [derived] source.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for stale lock threshold derivation

Tests cover:
- Default derivation produces correct value (50 min from 2700s coder)
- Picks max of coder/fixer/spec_review timeouts
- Changing coder timeout changes stale threshold
- Explicit AUTOPILOT_STALE_LOCK_MINUTES override takes precedence
- Dead PID and empty PID always detected as stale
- log_effective_config shows [derived] vs [env] vs [autopilot.conf]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update STALE_LOCK_MINUTES to document derived default

The default is now derived from max(TIMEOUT_CODER, TIMEOUT_FIXER,
TIMEOUT_SPEC_REVIEW) / 60 + 5, preventing race conditions at timeout
boundaries. Explicit override is still supported.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR review feedback for stale lock derivation

1. Use ceiling division ((timeout + 59) / 60) to guarantee full 5-minute
   buffer even for non-exact timeout values (e.g. 2999s → 55 min, not 54)

2. Rewrite "explicit override takes precedence" test to use a live PID
   with an aged lock file, proving the override threshold is actually used
   (previously passed trivially due to dead PID short-circuit)

3. Rewrite "uses derived threshold" test to validate derivation matters
   by testing the same aged lock against two different derived thresholds
   (previously passed trivially with fresh lock + live PID)

4. Add ceiling division test for non-exact timeout values

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix: reference CACHEBUST in RUN to actually bust Docker layer cache

The ARG CACHEBUST was declared but never used in any instruction,
so Docker never busted the cache for subsequent COPY layers. Adding
RUN echo ensures all layers after it rebuild when CACHEBUST changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: zodiac sign keys — return English lowercase instead of Russian

ZODIAC_SIGNS in astrology_calculator.py changed:
- Before: ["Овен", "Телец", ...] — Russian names returned from API
- After:  ["aries", "taurus", ...] — English lowercase, matches frontend SIGN_NAMES

Root cause: frontend SIGN_NAMES mapping uses English keys (aries/taurus/..),
but backend was returning Russian strings. SIGN_NAMES lookup always returned
undefined, falling back to raw Russian value. Worked for most signs via
fallback, but could show stale/wrong values (like "Печь", "стейк") from
old cached responses when keys didn't match.

Also updated example values in natal_chart.py schema for consistency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix design engine accuracy: AS1210 formula corrections and data flow improvements

Critical fixes:
- C1: Torispherical head denominator corrected from -0.5P to -0.2P (AS1210 Cl 3.12.5.2)
- C2: Flat head formula rewritten to t=d·C·√(P/f) per AS1210 Cl 3.15 (was 5.7× over-designing)
- C4: Refactored _calc_head() to delegate to validated standalone modules (head_se, head_tori, head_hemi, head_flat) with two-pass sizing approach

High-priority fixes:
- H1: Nozzle quantity now passed from backend (was hardcoded to 1)
- H3: Operating/test weight uses actual head volumes instead of hemisphere approximation
- H4: Flange weight uses DN/Class lookup table instead of linear approximation
- H5: Weld joint categories corrected (circumferential seams = Category B, not A)
- H6: Material fallback warnings surfaced to user (previously silent)
- M2: Flange rating values passed through from backend flange checks

Accuracy improvements:
- M1: Added circumferential stress check (Cl 3.7.4) alongside hoop stress (Cl 3.7.3)
- M3: Wind loading includes 200mm insulation allowance for hot/cold service vessels
- M4: Seismic notes flag simplified period estimation, warn for slender vessels (L/D>10)

Backend: 68 tests passing. Frontend: TypeScript clean, Vite build clean.

commit.message:
fix: push new branches Claude creates but doesn't push (#88)

* fix: skip OAuth token refresh when Claude workflows are active

Checks for in-progress Claude Code workflow runs before refreshing
the token. If any are running, the refresh is skipped to avoid
invalidating the access token mid-session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: push new branches Claude creates but doesn't push

Previously, if Claude created a new branch locally but didn't push it,
the safety-net step silently skipped the push because origin/<branch>
didn't exist. This caused "branch does not exist remotely" errors and
lost all work when the runner shut down.

Now checks if the remote branch exists first. If not, pushes the new
branch with -u to create it on the remote.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
AI prompt: every forwarded email is an instruction, always create task

Golden rule: never return empty actions unless genuinely spam/marketing.
Subject line IS the task — any name, time, or action creates a task.
Not limited to solar-specific keywords. "John 3pm" = task.
Only skip: bulk marketing, automated notifications, OOO replies.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve customer IDs to entity names in vessel audit log

- adapter.ts: look up entity name from DB when logging Customer field
  changes, so new entries store names instead of raw UUIDs
- VesselDetail.tsx: VesselHistoryView now resolves any UUID-valued
  Customer entries at render time (covers legacy entries already saved
  as UUIDs) via a lazy getEntities() call on mount
